title: 81 ruff lint issues (mostly UP045 Optional->| None)
evidence: ruff check *.py: 81 errors — 69 UP045 (use `X | None`), 6 E501 (e.g. test_soccer.py:442 line 98>88), 2 I001 import-order, 2 UP037, 1 UP035, 1 UP015; 75 autofixable. Matches code_quality=0.6667 in scores.json.
suggestion: Run `ruff check --fix` and re-format long lines; modernize Optional[...] to `... | None`.

title: Low maintainability index — large modules and 3 over-complex functions
evidence: maintainability=0.2880 in scores.json; soccer_data.py 589 LOC, soccer_queries.py 632 LOC; ruff C901: find_matches (complexity 17>10) soccer_queries.py:77, best_record (12) :494, team_record (11) :202.
suggestion: Extract the per-match filter predicates in find_matches into small helpers; split the data layer's normalization tables from the loader.

title: server.py only 64% covered — MCP tool wrappers and stdio entrypoint not exercised
evidence: coverage report: server.py 66 stmts, 24 missed, 64%; the test_mcp_* tests cover list_tools + one tool, but most @mcp.tool() wrappers, _selftest() and mcp.run() are untested. Overall coverage still 92% (test_coverage=0.92).
suggestion: Add a parametrized test invoking each server.py tool wrapper, or accept that wrappers are trivial pass-throughs to the tested engine.

title: Robust team-name normalization beyond spec
evidence: soccer_data.py:73-114 — ambiguous bases (Atlético-MG/PR/GO) kept distinct, _FULL_NAME_ALIASES and accent/suffix stripping; tested in test_soccer.py:101-156.
suggestion: None — exceeds the spec's name-variation requirement; surfaced for cross-run comparison.

title: Tests anchored to verifiable historical facts, not just smoke checks
evidence: test_soccer.py:321-347 asserts 2019 Brasileirão champion Flamengo 90 pts (28W/6D/4L) and 6 other known champions; 22 parametrized sample-question cases (>=20 required).
